name: Eggplant Potato Curry w/ Basmati and Peas
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon black onion seeds + 2 Tbsp for garnish
2 whole new potatoes, unpeeled and diced
2 tablespoons hot mango pickle (or 2 tablespoons apple cider vinegar)
1/3 cup flat leaf parsley, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Cut the eggplant into large cubes and sprinkle with salt. Place in a colander for 20 minutes.
3. Toast the onion seeds in the oven until fragrant.
4. In a heavy saucepan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over low heat. Add the toasted onion seeds, mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and ginger. Cook on low heat until fragrant.
5. Add potatoes and carrots to the pan and brown lightly, stirring frequently.
6. Add onion, garlic, cayenne pepper, salt, curry powder, and garum masala. Stir and cook on low heat for 5 minutes.
7. Add coconut milk, stewed tomatoes, and brown sugar. Stir well, then cover and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
8. In a large skillet, heat remaining olive oil over medium heat. Add the eggplant cubes to the hot oil and brown. Add lime juice and cook until just heated through, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and add to the curry mixture along with the mango pickle, basil leaves, jalapenos, and cauliflower.
9. Continue cooking for an additional 15 minutes.
10. To cook basmati rice, bring water to a boil in a medium pot. Add rice, bringing it back to a boil, then lower heat, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes. Stir once during cooking.
11. Add peas to the cooked rice and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
12. When potatoes are tender, turn off the heat. Serve curry over rice in bowls, creating a well in the center. Spoon some curry into the well and garnish with chopped parsley, remaining basil, black onion seeds, lime juice, fresh papaya slices, and cooked cauliflower florets.
